statute: Connecticut General Statutes § 20-418 et seq. (Home Improvement Act); § 20-417a et seq. (New Home Construction)
license_label: CT Home Improvement Contractor Registration Number
license_notes: Home improvement contractors must register with CT Department of Consumer Protection. GC must be registered as a major or minor (home improvement) contractor.

mandatory_clauses.label: CT HIC registration disclosure
Required contract elements
mandatory_clauses.text: This contract includes: contractor registration number, project description, timeline (start date, completion date, cancellation date), cost, payment method and schedule, and description of materials.

mandatory_clauses.label: 3-day right to cancel
mandatory_clauses.text:
RIGHT TO CANCEL: You have the right to cancel this contract within three business days of signing. To cancel, provide written notice to:
{{contractor_name}}, {{contractor_address}}

mandatory_clauses.label: Home Improvement Guaranty Fund notice
mandatory_clauses.text: HOME IMPROVEMENT GUARANTY FUND: Connecticut maintains a Home Improvement Guaranty Fund to compensate homeowners for losses due to contractor violations. Contact CT Department of Consumer Protection for information.

mandatory_clauses.label: Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: 1st violation $1,000, 2nd $1,500, 3rd $3,000 (DCP enforcement). Missing registration = contractor cannot enforce contract.
notes: Connecticut requires all home improvement contractors to register with DCP. Written contract required for jobs over $200. Contract must include HIC registration number, project description, timeline, cost, and payment method. 3-day right to cancel. Specialty trades (electrical, plumbing) require state licenses.
